member.name: Jayne Kirkham
member.synopsis: Jayne Kirkham is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Truro and Falmouth , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Batteries: Storage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the compliance of battery energy storage systems with government guidance, particularly concerning risk assessments and emergency planning.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Health and Safety Executive (HSE) oversees the legal framework setting the requirements for battery developers to manage risks throughout a grid-scale battery’s lifecycle. Government publishes guidance to support meeting these requirements, helping de...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of further changes to the Zero Emission Vehicle mandate on levels of investor confidence in the automotive and chargepoint sectors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to phasing out new cars relying solely on internal combustion engines by 2030 and to all new car and van sales being zero emission by 2035. These commitments provide the automotive and charging sectors with crucial investment c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Trust Ports: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what funding is available to restore and maintain trust ports.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Trust ports are commercial entities and have a range of funding options in line with other commercial bodies. The department does not provide general funding for the port sector, but is supporting the National Wealth Fund which has committed £5.8bn to fiv...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Multiple Myeloma
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many hospital trusts treat people with multiple myeloma; and how many patients they have treated in 2025, 2024, 2023, 2022 and 2021.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold this data in the requested format. We do hold data on cancer diagnosis and treatment, including for myeloma, which can be found at the following link:https://nhsd-ndrs.shinyapps.io/rcrd/We will build directly on the 10-Year He...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Retail Trade: Urban Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to support high street businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It was a pleasure meeting my honourable friend in April to discuss challenges facing small businesses in Truro and Falmouth.Our Plan for Small Businesses puts high streets at the heart of economic renewal. We are reforming licensing, tackling retail crime...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Disabled Facilities Grants: Social Rented Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he has considered making Disabled Facilities Grants available to social housing tenants due to the long delays they are facing for adaptations due to the depletion of the
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local authorities have a statutory duty to provide Disabled Facilities Grants within statutory timescales to eligible council and other social housing tenants in the same way as any other applicant. Government funding for the Disabled Facilities Grant fun...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Temporary Accommodation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to help local authorities reduce their number of out of area placements in temporary accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Legislation and statutory guidance is clear that local authorities should, wherever possible, place homeless households within their own area. However, in some areas where there is a limited supply of suitable accommodation, it is sometimes necessary to p...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Digital Technology: Infrastructure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment his Department has made of the adequacy of the role of the National Planning Policy Framework in supporting the deployment of digital infrastructure.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 124377 on 17 April 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Aerials: Registration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether her Department has assessed the potential merits of establishing a national register of mobile mast sites that periodically reports on their number and operational status, inclu
writtenQuestions.answerText: No assessment has been made on the merits of establishing a register of mobile mast sites. However, Ofcom’s Map Your Mobile tool, which was launched in June 2025, helps consumers to determine which mobile network provides the best level of service in thei...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Youth Services: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, regarding the Better Youth Spaces Fund, what the timeline is for Phase 2; what the criteria will be; and whether it will include rural and coastal areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Better Youth Spaces programme is currently in the design and development stage. Further details on the eligibility criteria and place selection will be announced in due course.The Department for Culture, Media and Sport is fulfilling its youth funding...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Childcare
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps HMRC takes to publicise closing dates for applications for funded childcare.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no closing dates for application for the Department for Education’s (DfE) funded hours offer. Parents must apply for a 30 hours code in the term before they want to take up a funded childcare place. This means that they must apply by: 31 August ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Buses: Carbon Emissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether businesses that re-power existing buses will be eligible for future government funding rounds for zero-emissions buses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to supporting the full transition to zero-emission buses (ZEBs). In March, we announced up to £73.2m in additional funding to support the delivery of approximately 484 ZEBs across 10 projects in England, on top of £38 million a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Buses: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the government plans to review procurement rules that prevent local authorities from prioritising UK-built buses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is determined to ensure that the £400bn spent annually on procurement benefits British industry, jobs, and skills.This government recently brought in The Local Government (Non-commercial Considerations) (England) Order 2026 so that local a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Educational Institutions: Adrenaline Auto-injectors
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has considered financing the provision of EpiPens for all children in an educational establishment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Mainstream school and high needs budgets have increased by £3.7 billion in 2025/26 compared to 2024/25. Overall schools funding will total £65.3 billion in the 2025/26 academic year (AY) compared to £61.6 billion in the 2024/25 AY. The Spending Review ann...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Students: Loans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of calculating student loan repayments on an annual basis so that people with fluctuating monthly incomes are not penalised and required to make larger repay
writtenQuestions.answerText: Student loan repayments for borrowers in the UK are collected via the tax system. This means employers deduct repayments from employees' pay checks for each pay period that their earnings exceed the relevant repayment threshold. If at the end of the year,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Rented Housing: Insulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the number of rural properties that will struggle to reach the minimum EPC C rating by the 2030 deadline for all rental properties.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government recognises there is no “one-size-fits-all” approach to tackling the UK’s diverse building stock. We have set out a range of provisions to ensure the cost and compliance burden is fair and proportionate for landlords. This includes a maximum spe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Multiple Sclerosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent discussions his Department has had with NHS England on expanding medical exemption criteria to include people with multiple sclerosis dependent on long-term medication.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are currently no plans to expand the medical exemption criteria to include people with multiple sclerosis dependent on long-term medication, and no recent discussions have been held with NHS England on this matter.People with multiple sclerosis may ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government wants operators to work collaboratively with site providers to reach agreements for siting mobile infrastructure. Masts may be removed or relocated for a range of reasons, and this is a commercial matter for the parties involved. The Depart...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
